page: Microsoft Azure L16s is a storage and memory-optimized virtual machine from the LS family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon E5-2698B v3 processor with an x86_64 architecture, providing 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs running at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The processor includes 40 MB of L3 cache and supports AVX, AES, and FMA instructions. The server is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core, and includes 2874 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability.

Performance benchmarks indicate average multi-core capabilities alongside weaker single-core metrics. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance falls into the bottom 10%, and the Geekbench single-core score is in the bottom 25%. However, multi-core benchmarks, including stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core, achieve average ratings. Memory performance is mixed; read-only small block cached bandwidth is average, whereas large-block uncached read and write bandwidths are weak and poor, respectively. Database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput all perform in the average tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the L16s offers a high density of local storage and memory relative to its compute resources, making it a viable option for workloads that require substantial local data caching without demanding high-frequency single-core CPU performance. Given the 128.0 GB memory capacity and 2874 GB of local storage, this server is suited for database operations, web serving, and moderate multi-threaded processing tasks. It is less suitable for single-threaded applications or large-scale LLM inference, where its prompt processing speeds for 70B models rank in the bottom 10%.
description: Microsoft Azure L16s is an x86_64 storage-optimized virtual machine powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2698B v3 processor. It delivers 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz, paired with 128.0 GB of RAM and 2874 GB of local storage. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, the server features 40 MB of L3 cache and supports AVX and AES instructions. Benchmark data shows average multi-core CPU and database performance, though single-core execution and large-block memory write bandwidth are weak. With a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core and substantial local storage, the L16s is designed for database operations, static web serving, and multi-threaded workloads that benefit from localized data caching rather than intensive single-core compute.
og_description: Microsoft Azure L16s is an x86_64 virtual machine featuring 16 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB of RAM, and 2874 GB of local storage, optimized for database workloads and multi-threaded applications.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure L16s is an x86_64 server with 16 Intel vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 2874 GB local storage, optimized for databases.
tagline: An x86_64 server combining sixteen dedicated cores, substantial memory density, and large local storage for data-intensive workloads.

128.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core
2874 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Average multi-core CPU, database, and static web serving performance
